headline: Survey at 1% completion: urgent acceleration needed across 7 inactive regions.
collection_status: 15 responses collected against 1,350 target represents critical underperformance at survey midpoint equivalent. Only 3 of 10 regions active; Kigoma, Mara, Songwe account for 100% of responses. Current pace will yield ~30 responses if continued; requires 45x acceleration.
key_findings: Volunteer-heavy sample (60%) risks bias; only 3 RC and 2 ARC respondents limit institutional perspective on policy and implementation capacity.
Stakeholder appetite strong: 73% prioritize new programs or KiuFunza expansion; scale readiness (2.7/10) severely lags ambition, signaling capability gaps.
Policy integration emerging priority: 3 coded responses reference government alignment and education sector policy inclusion; absent from top priorities suggests grassroots-leadership disconnect.
Partner satisfaction paradox: 4.6/10 satisfaction coexists with expansion requests, indicating stakeholders want more despite current service concerns.
flags: 7 regions (70% of target) have zero representation; regional equity and generalizeability at risk.
Scale readiness score of 2.7 contradicts expansion consensus—stakeholders may lack realistic understanding of operational constraints or resource requirements.
Sample skewed toward volunteers; RC/ARC underrepresentation limits data on institutional readiness, policy feasibility, and district-level constraints.
recommendation: Immediately deploy targeted mobilization to the 7 inactive regions with emphasis on recruiting RC and ARC respondents to balance sample. Conduct rapid in-depth interviews (5–10) with district officials on scale readiness gaps to diagnose whether low scores reflect training needs, resource shortage, or misaligned expectations.
